sections.heading: Complications

cards.name: Metastatic disease
cards.detail: Systemic therapy with TKIs; surgery or interventional procedures for limited disease [GIST-4, GIST-5]. Liver and peritoneal metastases are most common; lymph node metastases are rare except in SDH-deficient GIST [MS-2].

cards.name: Tumor rupture or hemorrhage
cards.detail: Emergency surgery; considered as metastatic disease due to high recurrence risk [GIST-3, GIST-C]. Tumor rupture is a poor prognostic factor [v,52].

cards.name: GI obstruction or perforation
cards.detail: Surgical intervention [GIST-C]. May present as acute abdomen requiring immediate attention [MS-2].

cards.name: Fluid retention
cards.detail: Common adverse events include fluid retention, diarrhea, nausea, fatigue, muscle cramps, abdominal pain, and rash.

cards.detail: Common adverse events include fluid retention, diarrhea, nausea, fatigue, muscle cramps, abdominal pain, and rash.

cards.name: Liver function test abnormalities
cards.detail: Serious toxicities: liver function test abnormalities (<5%), lung toxicity, neutropenia, GI bleeding, and cardiotoxicity (grade 3/4 in 8.2%) [59,60].

cards.name: Lung toxicity
cards.detail: Serious toxicities: liver function test abnormalities (<5%), lung toxicity, neutropenia, GI bleeding, and cardiotoxicity (grade 3/4 in 8.2%) [59,60].

cards.name: Neutropenia

cards.name: GI bleeding

cards.name: Cardiotoxicity

cards.name: Fatigue
cards.detail: Common toxicities: fatigue, nausea, vomiting, hematologic toxicities, diarrhea, hand-foot skin reaction (HFSR), hypertension, hypothyroidism, and cardiotoxicity.

cards.name: Hand-foot skin reaction (HFSR)
cards.detail: HFSR occurs in up to 20% of patients [65]; hypertension in 28% and hypothyroidism in 12% [68].

cards.name: Hypertension
cards.detail: HFSR occurs in up to 20% of patients [65]; hypertension in 28% and hypothyroidism in 12% [68].

cards.name: Hypothyroidism
cards.detail: HFSR occurs in up to 20% of patients [65]; hypertension in 28% and hypothyroidism in 12% [68].

cards.name: Diarrhea
cards.detail: Similar TKI-related toxicities, with diarrhea, HFSR, and hypertension being common [88].

cards.name: Alopecia
cards.detail: Common adverse events include alopecia, diarrhea, nausea, and fatigue; serious potential for secondary malignancies [117].

cards.name: Tumor rupture
cards.detail: Risks include tumor rupture, spillage, bleeding, infection, and anastomotic leak.

cards.name: Spillage
cards.detail: Risks include tumor rupture, spillage, bleeding, infection, and anastomotic leak.

cards.detail: Risks include tumor rupture, spillage, bleeding, infection, and anastomotic leak.

cards.detail: Risks include tumor rupture, spillage, bleeding, infection, and anastomotic leak.

cards.name: Anastomotic leak
cards.detail: Risks include tumor rupture, spillage, bleeding, infection, and anastomotic leak.

cards.name: Local tissue damage
cards.detail: Palliative radiotherapy for symptomatic lesions, especially bone metastases; category 2B recommendation [GIST-5]. May cause local tissue damage.
